name: partner-assisted chin-up
comment: Step up and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand grip. Bend the knees so a partner can assist from behind. Pull the body up until the elbows are at the sides or the chin is just above the bar. Lower until the arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: partner-assisted pull-up
comment: Step up and grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip. Bend the knees so a partner can assist from behind. Pull the body up until the chin is just above the bar. Lower until the arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: trap bar shrug
comment: Step into the trap bar and stand holding the handles at the sides. Raise the shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: cable shrug
comment: Stand facing the low pulley and grasp the cable bar with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand grip. Stand close to the pulley. With the arms straight, raise the shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: cable isolateral shrug
comment: Stand between the two low pulleys and grasp the stirrups at each side. Stand upright with the arms straight down. With the arms straight, raise the shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: dumbbell shrug
comment: Stand holding the dumbbells at the sides. Raise the shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: lever seated shrug (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the bench and grasp the lower handles at each side. Sit upright. Raise the shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: lever shrug
comment: Stand holding the lever bar with a shoulder-width overhand or mixed grip. Raise the shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: sled gripless shrug
comment: Stand with the shoulders under the padded bar. Raise the shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: smith shrug
comment: Grasp the Smith bar with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand grip. Unrack the bar by rotating it back. Raise the shoulders as high as possible. Lower, rack the bar, and repeat.

name: inverted shrug (on parallel bars)
comment: Stand between the parallel bars. Squat down and grasp the bars from above. Kick the legs up, inverting and balancing the body upside down. Keep the legs bent or straight, positioned vertically. Raise the body as high as possible by pulling the shoulders towards the ears. Lower and repeat.

name: suspended inverted shrug
comment: Grasp a set of gymnastics rings, one in each hand. Position beneath the rings and kick the legs up, inverting and balancing the body upside down. Raise the body as high as possible by pulling the shoulders towards the ears. Lower and repeat.

name: cable standing shoulder external rotation
comment: Stand sideways to a cable pulley set at elbow height. Grasp the stirrup with the far arm. Pin the elbow to the side with the forearm across the belly. Pull the stirrup away from the body as far as possible by externally rotating the shoulder. Return and repeat. Turn around and repeat with the other arm.

name: dumbbell lying shoulder external rotation
comment: Lie on one side with the legs slightly separated for support. Grasp a dumbbell and pin the elbow to the side with the forearm across the belly. Lift the dumbbell by externally rotating the shoulder. Return and repeat. Turn over and repeat with the other arm.

name: dumbbell upright shoulder external rotation (with support)
aliases: dumbbell upright shoulder external rotation (with support)
comment: Stand or sit with the side against a chest-height platform. Holding a dumbbell, place the upper arm flat on the platform with the elbow bent so the forearm is upright. Lower the dumbbell forward by rotating the shoulder. Return and repeat. Switch arms.

name: lever isolateral shoulder external rotation (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral shoulder external rotation (plate loaded)
comment: Lie supine on the bench nearest the pads. Position the body so the shoulders and elbows are aligned with the fulcrum on each side. Place the wrists or the backs of the hands against the upper set of pads on each side. Pull both lever arms back and down as far as possible to each side of the head by simultaneously rotating the upper arms. Return and repeat.

name: lever shoulder external rotation
comment: Place the elbow on the forearm pad and grasp the handle with the upper arm at the side and the forearm against the body. Pull the lever away from the body as far as possible. Return and repeat. Adjust the lever to the opposite side and repeat with the other arm.

name: suspended shoulder external rotation
comment: Grasp the suspension handles and position the bent elbows at each side of the waist. Step back until the straps are taut. Step forward slightly so the body reclines behind the handles, keeping the body and legs straight. Pull the handles apart to each side, maintaining the fixed elbow position throughout. Rise forward until the handles are at each side of the body. Return until the handles come back together in front of the body. Repeat.

name: cable standing shoulder internal rotation
comment: Stand sideways to a cable pulley set at elbow height. Grasp the stirrup with the near arm. Pin the elbow to the side with the elbow bent to approximately 90°. Pull the stirrup towards the body by internally rotating the shoulder until the forearm is across the belly. Return and repeat. Turn around and repeat with the other arm.

name: dumbbell shoulder internal rotation (on floor)
comment: Lie on a mat and hold a dumbbell in one hand. Position the upper arm flat on the mat close to the body, with the elbow bent to approximately 90° and the forearm held upright. Maintaining the bend in the elbow, lower the dumbbell towards the floor until a slight stretch is felt in the shoulder. Internally rotate the shoulder to raise the dumbbell back until the forearm is vertical. Repeat, then switch arms.

name: lever isolateral shoulder internal rotation (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral shoulder internal rotation (plate loaded)
comment: Lie supine on the bench furthest from the pads. Position the body so the shoulders and elbows are aligned with the fulcrum on each side. Place the wrists or hands against the lower set of pads on each side of the head. Pull both lever arms upward and towards each side of the torso as far as possible by simultaneously rotating the upper arms. Return and repeat.

name: lever shoulder internal rotation
comment: Place the elbow on the forearm pad and grasp the handle with the upper arm at the side and the forearm pointing away from the body. Pull the lever towards the body as far as possible. Return and repeat. Adjust the lever to the opposite side and repeat with the other arm.

name: machine-assisted chest dip
comment: Mount the wide dip bar with an oblique grip (bar diagonal under the palm), with the arms straight and the shoulders above the hands. Step down onto the assistance platform with the hips and knees bent. Lower the body by bending the arms, allowing the elbows to flare out to the sides. When a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders, push up until the arms are straight. Repeat.

name: machine-assisted chest dip (kneeling)
comment: Mount the wide dip bar with an oblique grip (bar diagonal under the palm), with the arms straight and the shoulders above the hands. Kneel on the padded platform, lowering it slightly so the hips are slightly bent. Lower the body by bending the arms, allowing the elbows to flare out to the sides. When a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders, push up until the arms are straight. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral lying fly
comment: Grasp the two opposing low pulley stirrups. Lie supine on a bench positioned in the middle and perpendicular to both pulleys. Slightly bend the elbows and internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows point back. Bring the stirrups together in a hugging motion, keeping the elbows fixed. Return to the starting position until a slight stretch is felt in the chest. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral standing fly
comment: Grasp the two opposing high pulley stirrups. Stand with the pulleys to each side. Lean forward slightly by flexing at the hips and knees. With the elbows slightly bent and the shoulders internally rotated so the elbows point back, bring the stirrups together in a hugging motion, keeping the elbows fixed. Return to the starting position until the chest muscles are stretched. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral bench press
comment: Lie on a bench and grasp the stirrups attached to the low cable pulleys on each side. Position the stirrups at the sides of the chest with the elbows under the wrists. Push the stirrups upward over each shoulder until the arms are straight and parallel. Return to the starting position until a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral chest press
comment: Sit on the seat and grasp the stirrups at each side. Position the elbows out to the sides, slightly lower than shoulder height, with the hands in front of the upper arms. Push the stirrups forward until the arms are straight and parallel. Return to the starting position until a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral standing chest press
comment: Stand between two shoulder-height pulleys, facing away from the cable columns. Grasp the stirrups from each side and position them at the sides of the chest with the elbows out. Adopt a split stance with one foot forward and one foot back, leaning slightly forward. Push the stirrups forward until the arms are straight and parallel. Return to the starting position until a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral decline chest press
comment: Sit on the seat and grasp the stirrups at each side. Position the elbows out to the sides, slightly lower than shoulder height, with the hands in front of the upper arms. Push the stirrups forward and slightly downward until the arms are straight and parallel. Return to the starting position until a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders. Repeat.

name: dumbbell decline bench press
comment: Sit on the decline bench with the feet secured under the leg brace and the dumbbells resting on the thighs. Lie back, bringing the dumbbells to the sides of the chest with the elbows under the wrists. Press the dumbbells upward until the arms are extended. Lower to the sides of the chest until a slight stretch is felt. Repeat.

name: dumbbell pullover
comment: Lie with the upper back across the bench, hips flexed slightly. Grasp a single dumbbell with both hands cupped under the inner plate. Hold it above the chest with the elbows slightly bent. Keeping the elbows slightly bent throughout, lower the dumbbell over and beyond the head until the upper arms are in line with the torso. Pull the dumbbell back over the chest. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral bench press (plate loaded)
comment: Lie on the bench with the chest under the lever bar. Grasp the lever bar with a wide oblique overhand grip. Press the bar until the arms are extended. Lower to the upper chest. Repeat.

name: lever chest dip (plate loaded)
comment: If available, set the handles to the wider position. Sit on the seat and grasp the handles with an oblique grip. Lean forward slightly. Push the lever down with the elbows pointing away from the body. Return until the chest is slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral chest press (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the seat with the chest at approximately the height of the handles. Grasp the handles with a wide overhand grip, elbows out to the sides just below the shoulders. Press the levers until the arms are extended. Return until the chest muscles are slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever decline chest press (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the seat with the lever grips at lower chest height. Grasp the grips with a wide overhand grip, elbows out to the sides just below the shoulders. Press the lever until the arms are extended. Return until the chest muscles are slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever chest dip
comment: If available, set the handles to the wider position. Sit on the seat and grasp the handles with an oblique grip. Lean forward slightly and allow the elbows to flare out. Push the lever down by extending the arms. Return with the elbows flaring out until the chest is slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral lying fly (gripless)
comment: Lie on the bench. Position both arms under the padded levers. Slightly bend the elbows and internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows point back. Bring the padded levers upward and together in a hugging motion. Return to the starting position until the chest muscles are stretched. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral seated fly
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the pad. Grasp the handles at each side at shoulder height. Slightly bend the elbows and internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows point backward. Move the handles forward and together until they meet in front. Return towards the starting position until a mild stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders. Repeat.

name: lever bench press
comment: Lie on the bench with the chest under the lever bar. Grasp the lever bar with a wide oblique overhand grip. Press the bar until the arms are extended. Lower to the upper chest. Repeat.

name: lever chest press
comment: Sit on the seat with the chest at approximately the height of the handles. If available, engage the foot lever to bring the levers within grasping range. Grasp the handles with a wide overhand grip, elbows out to the sides just below the shoulders. Release the foot lever. Press the lever until the arms are extended. Return until the chest muscles are slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral chest press
comment: Sit on the seat with the chest at approximately the height of the handles. Grasp the handles with a wide overhand grip, elbows out to the sides just below the shoulders. Press the lever until the arms are extended. Return until the chest muscles are slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral decline chest press
comment: Sit on the seat with the lever grips at lower chest height. Grasp the grips with a wide overhand grip, elbows out to the sides just below the shoulders. Press the lever until the arms are extended. Return until the chest muscles are slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: smith bench press
comment: Lie on a bench with the chest under the bar. Grasp the bar with a wide oblique overhand grip. Unrack the bar by raising and rotating it back. Lower to the chest. Press the bar until the arms are extended. Rack the bar and repeat.

name: smith decline bench press
comment: Lie on the decline bench with the feet secured under the leg brace and the lower chest under the bar. Grasp the bar with a wide oblique overhand grip. Unrack the bar by raising and rotating it back. Lower to the lower chest. Press the bar until the arms are extended. Rack the bar and repeat.

name: suspended self-assisted chest dip
comment: Set the suspension handles to approximately mid-thigh height. Grasp the handles at each side and straighten the arms with the shoulders above the hands. Lower the body by bending the arms back, allowing the knees to bend slightly and keeping the feet in contact with the floor for self-assistance as needed. When a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders, push the body up until the arms are straight. Repeat.

name: suspended chest press
comment: Grasp the handles and step forward between the suspension trainers. Position the arms downward and slightly forward, nearly parallel with the straps. Lean forward and step back onto the forefeet so the body is at the desired angle, with the arms straight and the torso in line with the legs. Lower the body by bending the arms, keeping the body straight and allowing the handles to separate slightly in line with the elbows as they flare out. Stop once a mild stretch is felt through the chest or shoulders. Push back up to the starting position, allowing the handles to travel inward until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: suspended fly
comment: Grasp the handles and step forward between the suspension trainers. Position the arms downward and slightly forward, nearly parallel with the straps. Lean forward and step back onto the forefeet so the body is at the desired angle, with the arms straight and the torso in line with the legs. Slightly bend the elbows and internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows point outward to each side. Lower the body by allowing the handles to separate outward until a mild stretch is felt in the chest. Bring the handles back together in a hugging motion. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral incline fly
comment: Sit on the seat and grasp the stirrups attached to the low cable pulleys. Lie back on the incline support. Position the stirrups at the sides of the chest with the elbows under the wrists. Press the stirrups upward until the arms are vertical. Bend the elbows slightly and internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows point out to the sides. Lower the stirrups outward to the sides of the shoulders, keeping the elbows fixed. When a stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders, bring the stirrups back together in a hugging motion above the upper chest. Repeat.

name: dumbbell incline fly
comment: Grasp two dumbbells and lie on an incline bench. Hold the dumbbells above the upper chest with the elbows slightly bent. Internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows point out to the sides. Lower the dumbbells outward to the sides of the shoulders, keeping the elbows fixed in the slightly bent position. When a stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders, bring the dumbbells back together in a hugging motion above the upper chest. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral incline chest press (on hammer military press, plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral incline chest press (on hammer military press, plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the seat with the upper chest just above the base of the handles. Grasp the handles with a wide overhand grip, elbows slightly lowered. Press the levers until the arms are extended. Return until the chest muscles are slightly stretched with the elbows out to the sides. Repeat.

name: lever incline bench press
comment: Lie on the incline bench with the chest under the lever bar. Grasp the lever bar with a wide oblique overhand grip. Press the bar until the arms are extended. Lower towards the chest. Repeat.

name: lever incline chest press
comment: Sit on the seat with the upper chest just above the grips. If available, engage the foot lever to bring the handles within grasping range. Grasp the handles with a wide oblique overhand grip and position the elbows out to the sides. Release the foot lever. Press the lever until the arms are extended. Return until the chest or shoulders feel slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral incline chest press
comment: Sit on the seat with the upper chest just above the grips. Grasp the grips with a wide overhand grip and position the elbows out to the sides. Press the lever until the arms are extended. Return until the chest muscles are slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral incline fly
comment: Lie on the bench and position the inside of the forearms under the roller pads with the arms out to each side. Slightly bend the elbows and internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows are directed back. Raise the levers upward and together in a hugging motion. Lower until a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders. Repeat.

name: smith incline bench press
comment: Lie on the incline bench with the chest under the bar. Grasp the bar with a wide oblique overhand grip. Unrack the bar by raising and rotating it back. Lower the bar to the chest. Press the bar until the arms are extended. Repeat.

name: barbell incline shoulder raise
comment: Lie on the incline bench and unrack the barbell with a shoulder-width overhand grip. Position the barbell over the upper chest with the elbows extended. Raise the shoulders towards the bar as high as possible. Lower the shoulders to the bench and repeat.

name: cable isolateral incline shoulder raise
comment: Sit on the seat and grasp the stirrups at each side. Push the stirrups out at 30° to 45° until the arms are straight and parallel to one another. Raise the shoulders towards the stirrups as high as possible. Lower the shoulders and repeat.

name: dumbbell incline shoulder raise
comment: Sit on the incline bench with the dumbbells resting on the lower thighs. Kick the dumbbells up to the shoulders and lean back. Position the dumbbells above the shoulders with the elbows extended. Raise the shoulders towards the dumbbells as high as possible. Lower the shoulders to the bench and repeat.

name: lever isolateral incline shoulder raise (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral incline shoulder raise (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the machine with the shoulders aligned with the lever grips. Grasp the lever grips with a shoulder-width overhand grip. Push the weight up until the arms are straight. Raise the shoulders towards the grips as far as possible. Lower the shoulders to the bench and repeat.

name: lever incline shoulder raise
comment: Sit on the machine with the shoulders aligned with the lever grips. Grasp the lever grips with a shoulder-width overhand grip. Push the weight up until the arms are straight. Raise the shoulders towards the grips as far as possible. Lower the shoulders to the bench and repeat.

name: smith incline shoulder raise
comment: Lie on the incline bench with the shoulders under the bar. Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip and extend the elbows. Unrack the bar by rotating it back. Raise the shoulders towards the bar as high as possible. Lower the shoulders to the bench and repeat.

name: cable kneeling crunch
comment: Kneel below the high pulley and grasp the rope attachment with both hands. Place the fists against the sides of the head. Sit the hips back and allow the cable tension to extend the torso until the spine is fully extended. With the hips stationary, flex the waist so the elbows travel towards the middle of the thighs. Return and repeat.

name: cable seated crunch
comment: Sit facing away from the medium-height pulley with the back against a back support. Grasp the rope attachment with both hands and place it securely over both shoulders. Allow the weight to extend the torso slightly. With the hips stationary, flex the waist so the elbows travel towards the hips. Return and repeat.

name: cable standing crunch (with isolateral straps)
comment: Stand with the back against the back pad, knees slightly bent, shoulders back, and chest high. Place both cable ropes over the shoulders, one in each hand, with the fists in front of each shoulder and the cables taut. Flex the waist so the torso pulls the ropes forward and downward. Return until the shoulders meet the back pad. Repeat.

name: dumbbell push crunch
comment: Lie on the steep incline bench with the feet hooked under the foot pad. Hold a dumbbell in each hand and position them directly above the shoulders. Flex the waist to raise the upper torso from the bench, keeping the lower back on the bench. Return until the shoulders contact the bench. Repeat.

name: lever lying crunch (plate loaded)
comment: Lie on the machine with the feet on the foot bar. Place the forearms on the bars at the sides, or grasp the handles above at each side. With the hips stationary, flex the waist to raise the upper pad. Return and repeat.

name: lever seated leg raise crunch (plate loaded)
aliases: lever seated leg raise crunch (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the back support and the lower legs under the padded bar. Grasp the handles above at each side. Pull the handles down while pulling the lower leg bar up by flexing the waist and pelvis. Return and repeat.

name: lever seated crunch
comment: Sit on the machine with the back and hips against the supports. If available, place the lower legs under the pads or on the platform. Grasp the handles above and position the backs of the arms against the pads at each side. With the hips stationary, flex the waist so the elbows travel downward. Return and repeat.

name: lever seated crunch (arm bar)
comment: Sit on the machine with the back and hips against the padded supports. Place the feet on the floor or foot bars so the thighs are approximately horizontal. Grasp the handles and position the forearms on the bars or pads. With the hips stationary, flex the waist so the arms and upper torso travel forward and downward. Return and repeat.

name: lever seated crunch (arm pad)
comment: Adjust the seat so the shoulders are at the same height as the padded lever. Sit on the machine and place the arms over the lever pad. Push the padded lever down slightly until the shoulders are fixed at approximately 90° or slightly greater. With the hips stationary, flex the waist in a "c" shape so the elbows point downward. Return and repeat.

name: lever seated crunch (chest pad)
comment: Adjust the seat so the chest is at the same height as the padded lever. Sit on the machine with the back of the hips against the hip pad. Place the hands on the outside of the lever pad. With the hips stationary, flex the waist in a "c" shape so the shoulders travel forward and downward. Return and repeat.

name: sled leg hip raise (ab coaster)
comment: Straddle the machine facing the handles. Grasp the handles and place the forearms on the pads. Place the shins on the padded sled with the knees forward and the feet hanging off the back. Sit back towards the heels without bending forward. Slide forward and upward by pulling the knees up, flexing the waist in a "c" shape. Return the sled down and back, arching the spine in the opposite direction while keeping the hips bent. Repeat.

name: weighted crunch
comment: Lie on a bench with the head hanging off and the knees and hips bent. Hold a plate behind the neck. Flex the waist to raise the upper torso from the bench, keeping the lower back on the bench. Return until the shoulders contact the bench. Repeat.

name: weighted crunch (plate on chest)
comment: Lie on a mat with the lower legs resting on a bench. Hold a plate on the chest with both hands. Flex the waist to raise the upper torso from the mat, keeping the lower back on the mat. Return until the shoulders contact the mat. Repeat.

name: weighted crunch (on stability ball)
comment: Sit on the exercise ball, then walk the feet forward until the shoulders and head hang off the back of the ball, with the knees and hips bent. Gently extend the back over the contour of the ball. Hold a plate behind the neck or on the chest, or use no weight. Flex the waist to raise the upper torso. Return to the starting position. Repeat.

name: weighted incline crunch
comment: Hook the feet under the support and lie on the incline bench with the hips bent. Hold a plate behind the neck, or clasp the hands behind the neck to use no weight. Flex the waist to raise the upper torso from the bench, keeping the lower back on the bench. Return until the shoulders contact the padded incline board. Repeat.

name: weighted incline crunch (arms crossed)
comment: Hook the feet under the foot brace and lie on the incline board with the hips bent. Hold a plate on the chest with both hands, or use no weight. Flex the waist to raise the upper torso from the board, keeping the lower back on the board. Return until the shoulders contact the padded incline board. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral standing twisting crunch (with straps)
aliases: cable isolateral standing twisting crunch (with straps)
comment: Stand with the back against the back pad, knees slightly bent, shoulders back, and chest high. Place both cable straps over the shoulders, one in each hand, with the fists in front of each shoulder and the cables taut. Flex and twist the spine so the torso pulls the straps forward and downward, rotating one shoulder towards the centre. Return until the shoulders meet the back pad. Repeat, alternating sides.

name: lever seated twisting leg raise crunch (plate loaded)
aliases: lever seated twisting leg raise crunch (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the back support and the lower legs under the padded bar. Grasp the handles above at each side. Pull the handles down while pulling the lower leg bar up and to one side by flexing and twisting the waist and pelvis. Return and repeat to the opposite side. Continue alternating between sides.

name: weighted incline twisting crunch (arms crossed)
comment: Hook the feet under the padding and lie on the incline bench with the hips bent. Hold a plate on the upper chest with both hands, or use no weight. Flex and twist the waist to raise the upper torso from the bench to one side. Return until the shoulders contact the padded incline board. Repeat to the opposite side, alternating sides.

name: weighted side crunch
comment: Lie on a mat with the legs together, knees and hips bent, and lower both legs to one side. Hold a weight on the opposite side of the head or across the upper chest, or use no weight. Flex the waist to raise the upper torso from the mat. Return until the shoulders contact the mat. Repeat. Reposition the legs to the opposite side and continue.

name: weighted twisting crunch
comment: Lie on a mat or bench with the knees and hips bent. Hold a plate behind the neck or on the chest with both hands. Flex and twist the waist to raise the upper torso to one side. Return until the shoulders contact the surface. Repeat to the opposite side, alternating sides.

name: weighted twisting crunch (on stability ball)
comment: Sit on the exercise ball, then walk the feet forward until the shoulders and head hang off the back of the ball, with the knees and hips bent. Gently extend the back over the contour of the ball. Hold a plate behind the neck or on the chest, or use no weight. Flex and twist the waist to raise the upper torso to one side. Return to the starting position. Repeat to the opposite side, alternating sides.

name: weighted incline twisting sit-up
comment: Hook the feet under the foot or ankle brace and lie on the incline bench with the hips and knees bent. Hold a plate behind the neck with both hands. Flex and twist the waist to one side while raising the torso from the bench. Return until the shoulders contact the padded incline board. Repeat to the opposite side, alternating sides.

name: weighted incline twisting sit-up (arms crossed)
comment: Hook the feet under the foot brace and lie on the incline board with the hips and knees bent. Hold a plate on the chest with both hands, or use no weight. Flex and twist the waist to one side while raising the torso from the board. Return until the shoulders contact the padded incline board. Repeat to the opposite side, alternating sides.

name: weighted twisting sit-up
comment: Secure the feet under a low stationary object and lie on a mat with the hips and knees bent. Hold a plate behind the neck with both hands. Flex and twist the waist to one side while raising the torso from the mat. Return until the shoulders contact the mat. Repeat to the opposite side, alternating sides.

name: weighted twisting sit-up (arms crossed)
comment: Secure the feet under a low stationary object and lie on a mat with the hips and knees bent. Hold a plate on the chest with both hands, forearms crossed. Flex and twist the waist to one side while raising the torso from the mat. Return until the shoulders contact the mat. Repeat to the opposite side, alternating sides.

name: suspended pull through
comment: Sit on the floor with the feet under the suspension trainer loops. Place the heels in the loops and extend the legs out straight. Place the hands on the floor at each side. Raise the hips from the floor, supporting the upper body with extended arms. Pull the hips back while flexing the spine in a "c" shape, then raise the hips by straightening the spine until fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable side bend
comment: Stand side-on to the low pulley and grasp the stirrup with the near hand, arm straight. Pull the stirrup by bending sideways at the waist so the torso moves away from the pulley. Lower by leaning the torso back towards the pulley. Repeat. Continue with the opposite side.

name: cable side crunch
comment: Stand side-on to the high pulley and grasp the stirrup with the near hand. Position the stirrup at the side of the shoulder, with the palm facing inward and the elbow down. Pull the stirrup downward by bending at the waist towards the cable column so the torso moves towards the base of the column. Return by leaning the torso away from the column, allowing the stirrup to rise. Repeat. Continue with the opposite side.

name: cable seated twist
comment: Straddle a bench positioned with one side facing the medium-height cable pulley. Grasp the stirrup with both hands and sit with the feet on the floor. Extend the arms straight towards the pulley. Keeping the arms straight, rotate the torso to the opposite side until the cable makes contact with the shoulder. Return to the starting position and repeat. Continue with the opposite side.

name: cable twist
comment: Grasp the stirrup of the shoulder-height cable pulley with both hands. Turn the body away from the pulley, stepping the feet wide apart and positioning the far foot furthest from the pulley. Raise the heel of the near foot slightly off the floor and bend both knees slightly. Interlace the fingers or place one hand over the other. Keeping the arms straight, rotate the torso to the opposite side until the cable makes contact with the shoulder. Return to the starting position and repeat. Continue with the opposite side.

name: dumbbell side bend
comment: Grasp a dumbbell in one hand with the arm straight at the side. Bend at the waist to the side opposite the dumbbell until a slight stretch is felt. Return to the opposite side the same distance. Repeat. Continue with the opposite side.

name: lever seated side leg raise crunch (plate loaded)
aliases: lever seated side leg raise crunch (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the machine with the seat turned to one side. Position the back against the back support and place the lower legs under the padded bar. Grasp the handles above at each side. Pull the handles down while pulling the lower leg bar up by flexing the waist and pelvis. Return and repeat. Turn the seat to the opposite side and repeat.

name: lever kneeling twist
comment: Adjust the range-of-motion setting on the machine to one side. Kneel on the padded stool with both knees between the leg pads. Place the torso against the upper pads and grasp the handles. Rotate the lower body through the waist to the opposite side. Return and repeat. Adjust the setting to the opposite side and repeat in the opposite direction.

name: lever seated twist
comment: Adjust the range-of-motion setting on the machine to one side. Sit with the legs against the padding. Place the torso against the pad and grasp the handles. Rotate the torso through the waist to the opposite side. Return and repeat. Adjust the setting to the opposite side and repeat in the opposite direction.

name: lever seated twist (gripless)
comment: Adjust the range-of-motion setting on the machine to one side. Sit with the legs straddled against the padding. Wrap the arms behind and under the padded bars at each side. Rotate the torso through the waist to the opposite side. Return and repeat. Adjust the setting to the opposite side and repeat in the opposite direction.

name: sled side leg hip raise (ab coaster)
aliases: sled side leg hip raise (ab coaster)
comment: Straddle the machine facing the handles, with the seat turned to one side. Grasp the handles and place the forearms on the pads. Place the shins on the padded sled with the knees forward and the feet hanging off the back. Sit back towards the heels without bending forward. Slide forward and upward by pulling the knees up, flexing the waist in a "c" shape. Return the sled down and back, arching the spine in the opposite direction while keeping the hips bent. Repeat. Turn the seat to the opposite side and repeat.

name: dumbbell one arm straight leg deadlift
aliases: dumbbell one arm straight leg deadlift
comment: Stand with a shoulder-width or wider stance. Grasp a dumbbell at one side and place the opposite hand at the side or behind the lower back. With the knees straight, lower the dumbbell between the feet by bending at the hips and rotating the torso so the shoulder of the weighted side is turned forward. Allow the hips to fall back as the dumbbell approaches the floor. Lift the dumbbell upward by extending the hips and torso until standing upright. Retract the shoulder if rounded. Repeat with the opposite arm.

name: hanging twisting leg raise
comment: Grasp and hang from a high bar with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand grip. Raise the legs to one side by flexing the hips and knees until the hips are completely flexed or the knees are well above the hips. Return until the hips and knees are extended downward. Raise the legs to the opposite side in the same manner. Continue, alternating between sides.

name: hanging twisting leg hip raise
comment: Grasp and hang from a high bar with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand grip. Raise the legs to one side by flexing the hips and knees until the hips are completely flexed. Continue to raise the knees towards the shoulder by flexing the waist to one side. Return until the waist, hips, and knees are extended downward. Raise the legs to the opposite side in the same manner. Continue, alternating between sides, until the spine is fully flexed to each side.

name: suspended side bend
comment: Grasp the handles and face one side of the suspension trainer anchor. Hold the handles above the head with the arms bent and step back until the straps are taut. Step forward slightly to achieve the desired lean, maintaining tension on the straps. Lower the hips away from the direction of the anchor by laterally flexing the spine. Return to the upright position and repeat. Reposition to face the opposite direction and continue with the other side.

name: suspended twisting jack-knife
comment: Sit on the floor facing the suspension trainer loops. Place the feet in the loops and turn to kneel on the hands and knees. Reposition the hands at shoulder-width or slightly wider from the suspension trainer so the body is square with the straps. With the arms straight, raise the knees from the floor so the body is supported by the arms and suspension trainer. Pull the knees towards one elbow by bending the hips and knees to one side until fully flexed. Return by extending the hips and knees. Repeat to the opposite side. Continue, alternating between sides.

name: barbell bent knee good-morning
comment: Position the barbell on the back of the shoulders and grasp the bar at each side. Bend at the hips to lower the torso forward until parallel to the floor, bending the knees slightly. Raise the torso until the hips are fully extended. Repeat.

name: barbell straight leg deadlift
comment: Stand with a shoulder-width or narrower stance on a shallow platform with the feet flat beneath the bar. Bend the knees and hinge forward with the lower back straight. Grasp the barbell with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand or mixed grip. Lift the weight to a standing position. With the knees straight, lower the bar towards the top of the feet by bending at the hips and waist. Lift the bar by extending the hips and waist until standing upright. Retract the shoulders if rounded. Repeat.

name: barbell hyperextension
comment: Position the thighs face down on the large pad and secure the lower legs under the padded brace. Place the barbell on the back of the shoulders and grasp the bar at each side. Raise the upper body until the hips and waist are fully extended. Lower the body by bending at the hips and waist until a mild stretch is felt or the torso is vertical. Repeat.

name: barbell 45° hyperextension
comment: Position the thighs face down on the padding and hook the heels on the platform lip or under the padded brace. Place the barbell on the back of the shoulders and grasp the bar at each side. Raise the upper body until the hips and waist are fully extended. Lower the body by bending at the hips and waist until a mild stretch is felt or the torso is approximately perpendicular to the legs. Repeat.

name: cable stiff leg deadlift
comment: Stand between two very low pulleys with a shoulder-width or narrower stance. Squat down and grasp the stirrup attachments at each side. Stand upright with the arms straight at the sides. Hinge forward by bending at the hips, bending the knees slightly and keeping the back straight. With the knees slightly bent, raise the torso by extending at the hips, gradually extending the knees until standing upright. Retract the shoulders if rounded. Repeat.

name: lever deadlift (plate loaded)
comment: Stand between the lever handles. Squat down with the feet flat and grasp the handles at each side. Lift the lever by extending the hips and knees to full extension. Retract the shoulders at the top if rounded. Return and repeat.

name: lever stiff leg deadlift (plate loaded)
comment: Stand between the lever handles with a shoulder-width or narrower stance. Squat down and grasp the handles at each side. Stand upright with the arms straight at the sides. Hinge forward by bending at the hips, bending the knees slightly and keeping the back straight. With the knees slightly bent, raise the torso by extending at the hips, gradually extending the knees until standing upright. Retract the shoulders if rounded. Repeat.

name: lever back extension
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the padded lever. Push the hips back against the seat by pressing the feet against the platform. Arch the back in a "C" shape, then extend the spine until fully extended. Return and repeat.

name: smith bent knee good-morning
comment: Position the bar on the back of the shoulders and grasp it at each side. Unrack the bar by rotating it back. Bend at the hips to lower the torso forward until parallel to the floor, bending the knees slightly. Raise the torso until the hips are fully extended. Repeat.

name: smith deadlift
comment: Stand with a shoulder-width or narrower stance with the feet flat beneath the bar. Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width or slightly wider mixed grip. Unrack the bar by rotating it back. Squat down by bending at the hips and knees. Lift the bar by extending the hips and knees to full extension. Retract the shoulders at the top if rounded. Return and repeat.

name: smith stiff leg deadlift
comment: Stand with a shoulder-width or narrower stance on an elevated platform with the thighs against the bar. Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand grip. Unrack the bar by rotating it back. With the knees slightly bent, lower the bar towards the top of the feet by bending at the hips, keeping the back straight. Lift the bar by extending at the hips until standing upright. Retract the shoulders if rounded. Extend the knees at the top if desired. Repeat.

name: weighted hyperextension
comment: Position the thighs face down on the large pad and secure the lower legs under the padded brace. Hold a weight plate to the chest or behind the neck. Raise the upper body until the hips and waist are fully extended. Lower the body by bending at the hips and waist until a mild stretch is felt or the torso is vertical. Repeat.

name: weighted 45° hyperextension
comment: Position the thighs face down on the padding and hook the heels on the platform lip or under the padded brace. Hold a weight plate to the chest or behind the neck. Raise the upper body until the hips and waist are fully extended. Lower the body by bending at the hips and waist until a mild stretch is felt or the torso is approximately perpendicular to the legs. Repeat.

name: bird dog
comment: Kneel on a mat on all fours with the hands and knees slightly apart. Extend one arm out straight level with the head while simultaneously raising and extending the opposite leg behind the body. Lower the arm and leg to the floor. Repeat with the opposite arm and leg. Continue, alternating between sides.

name: alternating bird dog
comment: Kneel on a mat on all fours with the hands and knees slightly apart. Extend the left arm out straight level with the head while simultaneously raising and extending the right leg behind the body. Lower the arm and leg to the floor. Repeat by raising the right arm and left leg in the same manner. Continue, alternating between sides.

name: 45° hyperextension (hands behind neck)
comment: Position the thighs face down on the padding and hook the heels on the platform lip or under the padded brace. Place the hands behind the neck or at the sides of the head. Raise the upper body until the hips and waist are fully extended. Lower the body by bending at the hips and waist until a mild stretch is felt or the torso is approximately perpendicular to the legs. Repeat.

name: superman
comment: Lie face down on a mat with the legs together and the arms extended out on the floor. Raise the upper body and legs off the floor simultaneously. Return the upper body and legs to the floor. Repeat.

name: contralateral superman
comment: Lie face down on a mat with the legs together and the arms extended out on the floor. Raise the left arm and right leg off the floor simultaneously. Lower the arm and leg to the floor. Repeat by raising the right arm and left leg in the same manner. Continue, alternating between sides.

name: rear bridge
comment: Sit on a mat with the knees bent and the feet flat on the floor. Place the hands on the floor at each side behind the body so the torso is reclined slightly. Raise the hips off the floor until the body forms a straight line from the knees to the shoulders. Hold the position, then lower and repeat.

name: incline rear bridge
comment: Sit on a mat with the legs straight and together. Place the hands on the floor at each side behind the body so the torso is reclined slightly. Raise the hips off the floor until the body is fully extended in a straight line. Hold the position, then lower and repeat.

name: barbell straight-back stiff-leg deadlift
comment: Grasp the barbell from a rack or the floor with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand or mixed grip. Stand with a shoulder-width or narrower stance. With the back straight, lower the bar towards the top of the feet by bending at the hips, bending the knees slightly. Lower the weight to the floor. With the knees still slightly bent, lift the bar by extending at the hips until standing upright. Retract the shoulders if rounded. Extend the knees at the top if desired. Repeat.

name: barbell rear lunge
comment: Position the bar on the back of the shoulders at upper chest height and grasp it at each side. Unrack the bar. Step back with one leg, planting the forefoot far behind on the floor. Lower the body by flexing the knee and hip of the supporting leg until the knee of the rear leg is almost in contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg and bringing the rear leg back alongside it. Repeat with the opposite leg, alternating between sides.

name: barbell single leg split squat
comment: Stand facing away from the bench. Position the bar on the back of the shoulders and grasp it at each side. Extend one leg back and place the top of the foot on the bench. Squat down by flexing the knee and hip of the front leg until the knee of the rear leg is almost in contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: barbell single leg squat
comment: Stand with the arms extended forward, holding the barbell. Balance on one leg with the opposite leg extended forward off the floor. Squat down as far as possible while keeping the elevated leg off the floor and the supporting knee pointed in the same direction as the foot. Return to the starting position until the knee and hip of the supporting leg are straight. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: barbell split squat
comment: Position the barbell on the back of the shoulders and grasp it at each side. Stand with the feet far apart, one foot forward and the other foot behind. Squat down by flexing the knee and hip of the front leg, allowing the heel of the rear foot to rise while the knee bends slightly until it almost makes contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: safety barbell squat
comment: Stand facing the safety barbell on the rack at upper chest height. Position the head in the yoke with the padded bar around the shoulders. Grasp the ends of the yoke or the safety bar out to the sides. Unrack the bar and step back. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend slightly forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Return to standing by extending the hips and knees until the legs are straight. Repeat.

name: barbell step-up
comment: Stand facing the side of a bench. Position the bar on the back of the shoulders and grasp it at each side. Place the foot of the first leg on the bench. Stand on the bench by extending the hip and knee of the first leg, then place the foot of the second leg on the bench. Step down with the second leg by flexing the hip and knee of the first leg. Return to the starting position by placing the foot of the first leg back on the floor. Repeat, alternating the leading leg between sides.

name: cable glute kickback
comment: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley and secure it to one ankle. Grasp the ballet bar with both hands and step far back with the other foot. Lean forward with the arms extended for support. Flex the cuffed leg at the hip and knee, then extend the hip and knee to kick back. Return the leg to the starting position and repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: cable rear lunge
comment: Stand between two very low pulleys with a shoulder-width or narrower stance. Squat down and grasp the stirrup attachments at each side, then stand upright with the arms straight at the sides. Step back with one leg, planting the forefoot far behind on the floor. Lower the body by flexing the knee and hip of the supporting leg until the knee of the rear leg is almost in contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg and bringing the rear leg back alongside it. Repeat with the opposite leg, alternating between sides.

name: cable split squat
comment: Stand between two very low cable pulleys and grasp the stirrup attachments at each side. Place one leg far forward and the other far back. Squat down by flexing the knee and hip of the front leg, allowing the heel of the rear foot to rise while the knee bends slightly until it almost makes contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg. Repeat. Continue with the legs in the opposite position.

name: cable squat
comment: Stand on the platform between two very low cable pulleys with a shoulder-width or slightly wider stance. Squat down and grasp the stirrups at each side with the arms straight. Keeping the chest high and back straight, raise the body by extending the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend slightly forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Return and repeat.

name: cable standing hip extension
comment: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley and secure it to one ankle. Grasp the ballet bar with both hands and step back with the other foot, keeping the arms straight. Hold the cuffed leg straight with the foot slightly off the floor. Extend the hip to pull the leg back. Return the leg to the starting position. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: cable bent-over hip extension
comment: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley and secure it to one ankle. Grasp the ballet bar with both hands and step far back with the other foot. Lean forward with the arms extended for support. Hold the cuffed leg slightly bent with the foot off the floor. Extend the hip to pull the leg back. Return the leg to the starting position and repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: cable step-up
comment: Stand behind the elevated platform with low cable pulleys at each side. Grasp the stirrups and stand upright with the arms straight at the sides. Place the foot of the first leg on the platform. Stand on the platform by extending the hip and knee of the first leg, then place the foot of the second leg on the platform. Step down with the second leg by flexing the hip and knee of the first leg. Return to the starting position by placing the foot of the first leg back on the floor. Repeat, alternating the leading leg between sides.

name: cable straight-back stiff-leg deadlift
comment: Stand between two very low pulleys with a shoulder-width or narrower stance. Squat down and grasp the stirrup attachments at each side, then stand upright with the arms straight at the sides. With the back straight, hinge forward by bending at the hips, bending the knees slightly during the descent. Raise the torso by extending at the hips while gradually extending the knees until standing upright. Retract the shoulders if rounded. Repeat.

name: dumbbell rear lunge
comment: Stand with the dumbbells at the sides. Step back with one leg, planting the forefoot far behind on the floor. Lower the body by flexing the knee and hip of the supporting leg until the knee of the rear leg is almost in contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg and bringing the rear leg back alongside it. Repeat with the opposite leg, alternating between sides.

name: split squat
comment: Hold a weight at the sides or in front of the body. Stand with the feet far apart, one foot forward and the other foot behind. Squat down by flexing the knee and hip of the front leg, allowing the heel of the rear foot to rise while the knee bends slightly until it almost makes contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: dumbbell split squat
comment: Stand with the dumbbells at the sides and the feet far apart, one foot forward and the other foot behind. Squat down by flexing the knee and hip of the front leg, allowing the heel of the rear foot to rise while the knee bends slightly until it almost makes contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: dumbbell squat
comment: Stand with the dumbbells at the sides. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend slightly forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Extend the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Repeat.

name: dumbbell front squat
comment: Grasp the dumbbells and clean them up to the shoulders so the side of each dumbbell rests on top of each shoulder. Balance the dumbbells by holding them with the elbows flaring outward. Position the feet shoulder-width or slightly narrower apart. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend slightly forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Extend the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Repeat.

name: dumbbell goblet squat
comment: Stand with a shoulder-width or wider stance. Hold the dumbbell by the inner plate at chest height with both hands. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Extend the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Repeat.

name: kettlebell goblet squat
comment: Stand with a shoulder-width or wider stance. Hold the kettlebell by the horns at chest height with both hands. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Extend the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Repeat.

name: dumbbell step-down
comment: Hold the dumbbells at the sides and stand with one foot on a bench. Position the foot to the side and forward of the supporting knee. Stand on the bench by straightening the supporting leg. Step down by returning the elevated foot to the floor. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever straight-back stiff-leg deadlift (plate loaded)
comment: Stand between the lever handles with a shoulder-width or narrower stance. Squat down and grasp the handles at each side. Stand upright with the arms straight at the sides. With the back straight, hinge forward by bending at the hips, bending the knees slightly during the descent. Raise the torso by extending at the hips while gradually extending the knees until standing upright. Retract the shoulders if rounded. Repeat.

name: lever lying leg press (plate loaded)
comment: Climb the steps to the top of the machine and lie on the horizontal padded platform with the shoulders against the pad. Place the feet equally spaced and slightly high on the platform. Grip the handles above the shoulder pads. Push the lever platform away by extending the hips and knees until the knees are straight. Return by flexing the hips and knees until just short of full flexion. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral seated leg press (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral seated leg press (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the padded support. Place the feet slightly high on the platform. Grasp the handles at the sides. Push the platforms away by extending the knees and hips until the knees are fully extended. Return until the hips are completely flexed. Repeat.

name: lever lying hip extension (plate loaded)
comment: Lie on the bench with the legs over the pads. Align the hip joints with the lever fulcrum and strap the hips down with the belt. Grasp the handles at each side. Extend the hips and knees until the legs are straight. Return by flexing the hips and knees. Repeat.

name: lever reverse hyper-extension (plate loaded)
comment: Place one ankle in the strap, then step up onto the side step of the apparatus with the other foot. Lay the torso on the bench and grasp the handles. Place the stepping foot in the other strap so the legs are side by side with the feet above the floor and the legs hanging straight down. Raise the legs by extending the hips as high as possible, keeping the legs nearly straight. Lower the legs to the starting position. Repeat.

name: lever single leg split squat (plate loaded)
aliases: lever single leg split squat (plate loaded)
comment: Stand between the lever handles and extend one leg back, placing the top of the foot on the cross bar. Squat down and grasp the handles at each side. With the chest high, lift the lever by extending the hips and knees to full extension. Return until the knee of the rear leg is almost in contact with the floor. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever single leg squat (plate loaded)
comment: Stand between the handles facing away from the fulcrum. Grasp both handles and stand upright. Cross the lower leg above the knee of the supporting leg. Bend the supporting knee slightly forward while allowing the hips to bend back, keeping the back straight and the knee pointed in the same direction as the foot. Squat down as low as possible. Extend the knee and hip until the leg is straight. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever split squat (plate loaded)
comment: Stand between the lever handles and place one foot behind on the floor with the forefoot down, and the other foot slightly forward. Squat down and grasp the handles at each side. With the chest high, lift the lever by extending the hips and knees to full extension. Return until the knee of the rear leg is almost in contact with the floor. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever squat (plate loaded)
comment: Squat down to place the shoulders under the padded lever. Place the feet shoulder-width apart, slightly in front of the shoulders. Extend the knees and hips until the legs are straight, then release the support lever. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend slightly forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Lift the lever by extending the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Repeat.

name: lever front squat
comment: Step onto the platform facing the padded levers. Squat down to place the shoulders under the padded lever. Place the feet shoulder-width apart on the platform. Extend the knees and hips until the legs are straight, then release the support lever if it does not disengage automatically. Lower by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend slightly forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Lift the lever by extending the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Repeat.

name: lever bent-over hip extension
comment: Step up onto the platform. Position the belly on the wide pad, the forearms on the padded arm rest, and grip the handles. Place the sole of one foot on the foot lever with the lower leg under the pad. Push the foot lever back by extending the hip. Return the leg to the starting position. Repeat. Reposition and continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever lying hip extension
comment: With the leg lever in the lower position, lie on the machine with the legs over the padded bar. Align the hip joints with the lever fulcrum and strap the hips down with the belt if available. Release the leg lever and adjust it until it is in the upper position. Grasp the handles at the sides. Push the padded leg bar down until the hips are extended. Return and repeat.

name: lever standing hip extension
comment: Stand on the platform facing to one side and grasp the bar for support. Place the leg nearest the machine against the padded roller while standing on the other leg. Push the lever back by extending the hip. Return until the knee is higher than the hip. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever standing hip extension (with abdominal pad)
aliases: lever standing hip extension (with abdominal pad)
comment: Stand on the platform with the front of the hips against the edge of the abdominal pad. Place the flexed leg over the padded roller while standing on the other leg. Grasp the handles with both hands. Push the lever back by extending the hip and straightening the leg. Return by flexing the hip and knee. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever seated leg press
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the padded support. Place the feet slightly high on the platform. Grasp the handles at the sides. Extend the knees and hips until the knees are fully extended. Return until the hips are completely flexed. Repeat.

name: lever bent-over glute kickback
comment: Lean over the padded surface and grasp the handles. Bend one knee and position the foot against the lever platform or pedal. Push the platform or pedal back by extending the leg. Return the leg to the starting position. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever kneeling glute kickback
comment: Kneel on the machine by placing one knee on the lower pad. Rest the torso on the middle pad, place the forearms on the padded arm rest, and grip the handles. Place the foot of the other leg against the lever platform. Push the platform back by extending the leg. Return the leg to the starting position and repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever standing glute kickback
comment: Step onto the platform with one foot and grasp the handles. Place the other foot on the lever platform. Push the platform back by extending the leg until straight. Return the leg to the starting position. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever squat
comment: Squat down to place the shoulders under the padded lever. Place the feet shoulder-width apart, slightly in front of the shoulders. Raise the lever by extending the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend slightly forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Return and repeat.

name: lever v-squat
comment: Position the shoulders under the shoulder pads with the back against the back pad. Place the feet on the platform, shoulder- or hip-width apart. Squeeze the hand lever and squat down by bending the hips and knees until just short of complete flexion. Release the hand lever and rise slightly on the sled until the weight stack is engaged. Raise the lever by extending the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Squat down with the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the knees and hips are near complete flexion. Repeat.

name: sled kneeling glute kickback
comment: Kneel on the machine by placing the knees on the lower pad and the feet against the bars to the rear if available. Place the forearms on the padded armrest and grip the handles. Position one foot so the middle of the sole is under the foot bar. Push the foot bar back by extending the leg as far as possible. Return the leg to the starting position and repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: sled standing glute kickback
comment: Straddle the sled and place one foot on the pedal. Place the chest on the pad and grasp the handles. Push the platform back by extending the leg until straight. Return the leg to the starting position. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: sled 45° leg press
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the padded support. Place the feet slightly high on the platform. Extend the hips and knees, then release the dock lever and grasp the handles at the sides. Lower the sled by flexing the hips and knees until just short of complete flexion. Return by extending the knees and hips. Repeat.

name: sled lying leg press
comment: Lie on the platform with the shoulders against the pad. Place the feet slightly high on the platform. Extend the hips and knees to press the sled. Return until the hips are completely flexed. Repeat.

name: sled seated leg press
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the padded support. Place the feet slightly high on the platform. Grasp the handles at the sides. Extend the knees and hips until the knees are fully extended. Return until the hips are completely flexed. Repeat.

name: sled squat
comment: Squat down to place the shoulders under the padded bars and lean back against the back pad if available. Place the feet shoulder-width apart, slightly in front of the shoulders. If a hand or lever lock is available, disengage the weight, squat to the lowest position, and re-engage the lock. Lift the sled by extending the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Lower the sled by allowing the knees to bend slightly forward while the hips bend back, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Return and repeat.

name: smith straight-back stiff-leg deadlift
comment: Stand with a shoulder-width or narrower stance with the thighs against the bar. Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand grip. Unrack the bar by rotating it back. With the knees slightly bent, lower the bar towards the top of the feet by bending at the hips, keeping the back straight. Lift the bar by extending at the hips until standing upright. Retract the shoulders if rounded. Extend the knees at the top if desired. Repeat.

name: smith rear lunge
comment: With the bar at upper chest height, position it on the back of the shoulders and grasp it at each side. Place the feet under the bar or slightly forward. Straighten the legs to lift the weight, then unrack the bar by rotating it back. Step back with one leg, planting the forefoot far behind on the floor. Lower the body by flexing the knee and hip of the supporting leg until the knee of the rear leg is almost in contact with the floor. Return to the starting position by extending the hip and knee of the forward leg and bringing the rear leg back alongside it. Repeat with the opposite leg, alternating between sides.

name: smith squat
comment: With the bar at upper chest height, position it on the back of the shoulders and grasp it at each side. Place the feet slightly forward. Unrack the bar by rotating it back. Squat down by bending the hips back while allowing the knees to bend slightly forward, keeping the back straight and the knees pointed in the same direction as the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Extend the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Repeat.

name: weighted reverse hyper-extension
comment: Place a sandbag or medicine ball between the ankles. Lay the torso on the bench and grasp the handles. Position the feet above the floor with the legs hanging straight. Raise the legs by extending the hips as high as possible, keeping the legs nearly straight. Lower the legs to the starting position. Repeat.

name: weighted single leg squat
comment: Stand with the arms extended forward, holding a medicine ball or weight plate. Balance on one leg with the opposite leg extended forward off the floor. Squat down as far as possible while keeping the elevated leg off the floor and the supporting knee pointed in the same direction as the foot. Return to the starting position until the knee and hip of the supporting leg are straight. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: suspended hip bridge
comment: Sit on the floor facing the suspension trainer loops. Grasp the bottom of the loops, then lie back. Raise the legs and place the heels in the loops with the soles contacting the handles. Lie on the mat with the knees and hips bent and the straps hanging vertically. Place the arms at the sides. Raise the hips by extending until fully extended. Lower the hips to the mat. Repeat.

name: suspended single leg squat
comment: Grasp the suspension handles and step back until the arms are extended forward and straight. Step forward so the body reclines behind the handles, keeping the arms straight and the shoulders back. Position the body and legs straight at the desired angle. Balance on one leg with the opposite leg extended forward off the floor. Squat down as far as possible while keeping the elevated leg off the floor and the supporting knee pointed in the same direction as the foot. Return to the starting position until the knee and hip of the supporting leg are straight. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: cable hip abduction
comment: Attach the cable cuff to the far ankle and stand facing to one side of the low pulley. Step away from the stack and grasp the ballet bar. Stand on the near foot and allow the far leg to cross in front. Raise the far leg to the opposite side by abducting the hip. Return and repeat. Turn around and continue with the opposite leg.

name: dumbbell lying hip abduction
comment: Lie on one side on a mat. Extend the top leg down straight and position the lower leg back underneath. Grasp the dumbbell with the hand furthest from the floor and position it on the upper side of the thigh. Raise the weighted leg as high as possible, keeping the dumbbell on the side of the thigh. Return the leg to the floor and repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever seated hip abduction (plate loaded)
comment: Stand near the side of the apparatus. Place one leg onto the far padded lever and sit on the seat. Lift the other leg onto the other padded lever. Lean back onto the back pad. Grasp the handles at the sides. Move the legs apart as far as possible. Return and repeat.

name: lever seated hip abduction
comment: Sit on the machine with the legs inside the side pads. If available, place the heels on the foot bars. Release and pull the lever brace to position the legs together, then engage the lever into the locked position. Lean back and grasp the bars at the sides. Move the legs apart as far as possible. Return and repeat.

name: lever standing hip abduction
comment: Adjust the platform so the lever fulcrum is at the same height as the hip. Set the roller to the low position. Face the machine and grasp the bars. Place the outside of one thigh against the roller pad and shift the body weight to the opposite leg. Raise the leg to the side by abducting the hip. Return and repeat. Reposition the roller pad lever and continue with the opposite leg.

name: weighted lying hip abduction
comment: Sit on a mat with the knees bent and load a bar with plates on one side. Place the bar on the outside of the foot with the weight plate positioned beyond the foot. Grasp the opposite end of the bar and lie on the side, with the weighted leg extended straight and the lower leg bent underneath. Raise the weighted leg as high as possible, balancing the bar on the side of the foot. Return to the floor and repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: suspended hip abduction
comment: Sit on the floor facing the suspension trainer loops. Grasp the bottom of the loops, then lie back. Raise the legs and place the heels in the loops with the soles contacting the handles. Extend the legs out and place the arms on the floor at the sides. Straighten the lower back, knees, and hips, raising the back and hips off the floor. Pull the legs apart as far as possible. Return by bringing the legs together. Repeat.

name: cable lying straight leg raise
comment: Attach the cable ankle straps to both ankles. Lie on a mat or bench, far enough back so the cable is taut. Grasp a support behind the head. With the knees straight, raise the legs by flexing the hips until the thighs are just past perpendicular to the torso, or before the hips rise off the mat. Return until the hips are extended. Repeat.

name: cable standing leg raise
comment: Attach the cable ankle cuff to one ankle. Stand facing away from the low pulley. Step forward with the opposite leg so the cuffed leg is pulled back. Grasp the lateral bars or another support. Raise the knee by flexing the hip, allowing the lower leg to bend back under the resistance. When the thigh is just beyond horizontal, lower the leg until the hip and knee are extended. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: lever hip flexion
comment: Stand on the platform facing to one side and grasp the bar for support. Place the front of the leg nearest the machine against the padded roller while standing on the other leg. Raise the lever by flexing the hip until the knee is higher than the hip. Return until the hip is extended. Repeat. Continue with the opposite leg.

name: weighted decline sit-up
comment: Sit on the apparatus with the lower leg secured under the padded bar. Hold a weight in front of the chest or behind the neck, or use no weight. Lower the body back until the hips are almost extended. Raise the body by flexing the hips until the torso is upright. Repeat.

name: weighted incline leg raise
comment: Sit on the incline board and place a weight between the feet, or use no weight. Lie on the incline board with the torso elevated. Grasp the foot hooks or the sides of the board for support. Raise the legs by flexing the hips and knees until the hips are completely flexed. Return until the hips and knees are extended. Repeat.

name: weighted lying leg raise
comment: Sit on the end of a bench and place a weight between the feet. Lie on the bench. Grasp the bench on each side behind the head for support. Raise the legs by flexing the hips and knees until the hips are completely flexed. Return until the hips and knees are extended. Repeat.

name: weighted roman chair sit-up
comment: Sit on the apparatus with the lower leg secured under the padded bar. Hold a weight in front of the chest or behind the neck, or use no weight. Lower the body back until the hips are almost extended. Raise the body by flexing the hips until the torso is upright. Repeat.

name: weighted seated leg raise
comment: Sit on the edge of a bench with the legs extended to the floor. Place a weight between the ankles and grasp the edge of the bench. Lean the torso back and balance on the edge of the bench. Raise the legs by flexing the hips and knees while pulling the torso slightly forward to maintain balance. Return by extending the hips and knees and leaning the torso back to counterbalance. Repeat.

name: weighted vertical leg raise
comment: Stand in the vertical leg raise apparatus. Place a weight between the ankles. Position the forearms on the padded parallel bars with the hands on the handles and the back against the vertical pad. Hang with the legs straight and the feet off the floor. Raise the legs by flexing the hips and knees until the hips are completely flexed or the knees are well above the hips. Return until the hips and knees are extended. Repeat.

name: weighted vertical leg raise (on parallel bars)
aliases: weighted vertical leg raise (on parallel bars)
comment: Stand between the parallel bars and place a weight between the ankles. Grip the bar on each side with an overhand grip. Lift the body off the floor and balance upright with the arms and legs straight. Raise the legs by flexing the hips and knees until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Return until the hips and knees are extended. Repeat.

name: weighted vertical straight leg raise
comment: Stand in the vertical leg raise apparatus. Place a weight between the ankles. Position the forearms on the padded parallel bars with the hands on the handles and the back against the vertical pad. Hang with the legs straight and the feet off the floor. With the knees straight, raise the legs by flexing the hips until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Return until the hips are extended. Repeat.

name: seated leg raise
comment: Sit on the edge of a bench with the legs extended to the floor. Place a weight between the ankles, or use no weight. Grasp the edge of the bench. Lean the torso back and balance on the edge of the bench. Raise the legs by flexing the hips and knees while pulling the torso slightly forward to maintain balance. Return by extending the hips and knees and leaning the torso back to counterbalance. Repeat.

name: trap bar squat
comment: Stand on the platform under the loaded trap bar.
